title: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery Workshop
content:
AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery Workshop AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (AWS DRS) helps minimize downtime and data loss with the ability to quickly and reliably recover applications on-premises and in the cloud using available storage. Reasonable cost, minimal computing resources, and point-in-time recovery.
This lab simulates an on-premises environment that allows customers to replicate disaster recovery to AWS.
The simulation environment is supported by Amazon EC2 instances and configured in a way suitable for many customer environments. The network includes private and public subnets, a DNS service, pre-installed applications, and a Windows host bastion server.
The image below is a basic network diagram for the AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery service (DRS). The simulation environment matches the service requirements.
Basic knowledge This is a lab for Solution Architects, SysOps, ServerOps, InfraOps, and other professionals.
To get the most out of this lab, you must be familiar with the following:
 Connect to Microsoft Windows server using Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Connect to Linux server using SSH  Main content:  Introduction Preparation steps Connect Bastion Host DRS Configuration Configuring DRS IAM user Install Agent EC2 Launch Template Failing Over Resource Cleanup

title: Introduction
content:
Introduction AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (AWS DRS) helps minimize downtime and data loss with the ability to quickly and reliably recover applications on-premises and in the cloud using available storage. Reasonable cost, minimal computing resources, and point-in-time recovery.
This lab simulates an on-premises environment that allows customers to replicate disaster recovery to AWS.
The simulation environment is supported by Amazon EC2 instances and configured in a way suitable for many customer environments. The network includes private and public subnets, a DNS service, pre-installed applications, and a Windows host bastion server.
The image below is a basic network diagram for the AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery service (DRS). The simulation environment matches the service requirements.
Basic knowledge This is a lab for Solution Architects, SysOps, ServerOps, InfraOps, and other professionals.
To get the most out of this lab, you must be familiar with the following:
 Connect to Microsoft Windows server using Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Connect to Linux server using SSH

title: Prepare the infrastructure
content:
Prepare infrastructure   In this lesson, we will perform the lab in Region US West (Oregon) us-west-2
  In this section, we will prepare the resources for the lab by instantiating the CloudFormation template.
  You download CloudFormation template here here
    Access the CloudFormation interface
 Select Create stack     In the Create stack interface
 Select Template is ready Select Upload a template file Select Next     For Stack name
 Enter EDRW For other parameters, use the default.    Select Next  Select Next  Make a selection of require capabilities, then select Create stack  About 30 minutes later, we will observe the interface and successfully create five stacks.  See Outputs of the stack. We will see if we create a BastionRDP used to connect.

title: Connect to the Bastion Host
content:
Connect to Bastion Host  To connect to the Windows Remote Desktop server, we can use many tools and software to support the connection.   For Windows: use Remote Desktop Connection For MacOS: use Remote Desktop 10 For Linux: use Remmina  For the Credentials part, we will use:
Bastion Hostuser = Administratorpassword = Adm1nP@sLinux Hostspassword=SshPass1   We access the EC2 interface
 Select MID-Bastion instance Select Connect     To connect, we will choose RDP client
 Select Connection type* as Connect using RDP client Select Download remote desktop file     After downloading, select Remote Desktop Connection
 Select Connect    Fill in Password  Select Yes to connect  Complete the connection.

title: Configure DRS Settings
content:
Perform DRS configuration This section will perform AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (DRS) configuration.
 Go to AWS Management Console  Find DRS Select AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery    The First step, we will choose Set default replication settings   In the Edit default replication settings interface
 Select CloudEndure Staging for Staging area subnet For Replication server instance type, select t3.small.     For Security group
 We will leave the default value.    Check again and select Save changes

title: Configure DRS Agent
content:
Configure DRS Agent IAM User To do this, we will create an IAM User
  Access the AWS Management Console interface
 Find IAM Select IAM    In the AWS IAM interface   Select User Select Add user   Create user with the name drs-agent-user
 Then, select Next:Permission     For Set Permissions
 Select Attach existing policies directly Find and select AWSElasticDisasterRecoveryAgentInstallationPolicy Select Next:Tags    Select Next:Review  Select Create user  We will download Download.csv to save Access Key information for the next steps.

title: Installing the Agent
content:
Install Agent   In the Bastion Host interface
 Select Putty Select wordpress-web Select Open    We will perform login with password as the connection part  Bastion Hostuser = Administratorpassword = Adm1nP@sLinux Hostspassword=SshPass1 Login successfully.  After successful login, we will install Agent  wget -O ./aws-replication-installer-init.py https://aws-elastic-disaster-recovery-us-west-2.s3.amazonaws.com/latest/linux/aws-replication-installer-init. py  Then, we will execute the command  sudo python3 aws-replication-installer-init.py  Make configuration
 AWS Region Name AWS Access Key ID AWS Secret Access Key For Choose the disks you want to replicate, select Enter    We will follow the same steps for the database server part.  Back to DRS source server page see initial sync process   Both DRS source servers
 Create security groups Launch replication server Boot replication server Authenticate with service Download replication software Create staging disks Attach staging disks Pair replication server with Agent Connect Agent with replication server Start data transfer    We will select 1 DRS source server to see the details.  You will have to wait about 10 minutes when 100% done  After about 1 minute, you will see Ready for recovery status as Ready and Data replication status as Healthy

title: Configure Launch Settings
content:
Configure EC2 Launch Template   In the DRS interface
 Select Source Servers Choose a source     In the source server interface
 Select Launch settings    For Launch Setting, select None  Check again, we will see Instance type right sizing is Off  Configure EC2 Launch template  For Template version description enter WorkshopTemplate  For Instance type, select t3.small   For Network Settings, select Target Private for Subnet
 For existing security group, select TargetSecurityGroup     About the configuration Configure Storage
 Select Volume type as gp3    Select Create template version  Create a template version successfully.  Details of templates.

title: Failing Over
content:
Failing Over   Go to the DRS page
 We will see there are 2 Hostname Choose 1 hostname     Next
 Select Initiate recovery job Select Initiate drill    For Points in time select Use most recent data  Select Initiate drill   Go to DRS interface, select Recovery job history
 View Job log of Hostname web    View Job log of Hostname database.  '
Go to the EC2 interface. You will see there are two instances of wordpress-db-onpremsim.env and wordpress-db.onpremsim.env

title: Clean up resources
content:
Clean up resources   Go to CloudFormation
 Select stack Select Delete    Select Delete stack
